spec: offices/pm/inbox/2026-04-29-from-spool-sprint19-consolidated.md (Spool's 4-drain + 3-drive consolidated note) + offices/pm/inbox/2026-04-29-from-ralph-post-deploy-system-health-drive4.md (Ralph's V-1 through V-7 server-side variances)
backlogItem: B-037 (Pi/server reconciliation) + B-047 versioning prerequisite (US-241/US-A) + Sprint 18 carryforward (US-216 fix US-234, US-228 fix US-236)

testBaseline.note: Post-Sprint-18 close baseline. Sprint 18 closure adds ~50 tests for US-226 through US-233. Sprint 19 baseline conservatively set at ~3200; verify at sprint start. main@d8583d3 (Sprint 18 code) + 477b62c (closeout) at sprint-load time.

sprintNotes: GOAL: fix what Sprint 18's 'tests pass / production fails' wave exposed (US-216 SOC ladder + US-228 drive_summary backfill, both broken in 4 drain tests + 3 drives despite passes:true) AND reconcile server-side schemas with Pi reality (Ralph's V-1 drive_summary + V-2 dtc_log gaps). Plus B-047 versioning prerequisite. After Sprint 19, the next drain test should actually fire stage transitions + populate battery_health_log; the next cold-start drive should produce drive_summary with non-NULL ambient/baro/battery; sync_history failures should drop from 60% to ~0%.

EXECUTION ORDER (P0 first, parallelize where deps allow): US-234 (US-216 VCELL trigger, M, no deps), US-235 (UpsMonitor BATTERY-detection, S, no deps; pairs with US-234 but can ship independently), US-236 (US-228 defer-INSERT, S, no deps), US-237 (V-1 drive_summary 0004 migration + namespace cleanup, M, no deps), US-238 (V-2 dtc_log 0005 migration, S, no deps). Then P1: US-239 (V-3 connection_log path-a/b, S/M), US-240 (V-5 server orphan backfill, S), US-241 (B-047 US-A versioning, S). All 8 stories independent — Ralph can pick any order.

THEME: Sprint 18 was the 'capture surface complete' sprint but shipped two stories with synthetic tests that didn't catch the production failure mode. Sprint 19 fixes both with stronger synthetic tests per the new feedback_runtime_validation_required rule, plus reconciles the server-side schemas to match Pi (V-1 + V-2). After Sprint 19, the foundation is genuinely trustworthy for the first time.

CONSTRAINTS: synthetic tests for US-234/US-236 MUST tightly mock the production runtime conditions (MAX17048.readVCell() stair-step for US-234; first-reading-arrival timing for US-236). If tests would pass against the OLD broken code, they are not strong enough — this is the Sprint 18 lesson. Live drills (drain test 5, cold-start drive 6) are POST-SPRINT ACTION ITEMS per the no-human-tasks-in-sprint rule.

GROUNDING SOURCES: Spool sprint19-consolidated 2026-04-29 + Ralph Drive 4 health check 2026-04-29 + 4 prior drain test datasets + B-047 backlog item + Sprint 18 closure docs + new feedback_runtime_validation_required rule.

POST-SPRINT-19 ACTION ITEMS (not in sprint.json per CIO rule, but tracked here for sprint-close verification): (1) CIO runs drain test 5 with US-234+US-235 deployed; verify stage transitions fire + battery_health_log populates. (2) CIO runs cold-start drive 6 with US-236 deployed; verify drive_summary row has non-NULL ambient/baro/battery. (3) CIO redeploys server with US-237+US-238 migrations; verify sync_history drive_summary failure rate drops to 0. (4) Spool runs review ritual against Drive 5 once V-1 lands metadata. (5) Spool updates knowledge.md with Drive 5 baseline + post-jump LTFT adaptation tracking. If any action item reveals a synthetic-test gap, file follow-up story for Sprint 20 — do NOT reopen this sprint's stories.

PARALLEL SPOOL DELIVERABLES tracked here (NOT Ralph stories): (a) MAX17048 calibration learning run procedure spec (B-048) — Spool authors, Ralph implements scripts in Sprint 20+; (b) UPS charge-path audit — Spool's 2-hour task, S deliverable; (c) Drive 5 baseline update in knowledge.md (post-V-1 + ritual).

stories.title: Fix US-216 trigger source: switch SOC% → VCELL thresholds (3.70/3.55/3.45V)
stories.intent: Across 4 drain tests over 9 days (Drain 1-4), the PowerDownOrchestrator shipped in Sprint 16 (US-216, 444 LOC, correct state-machine design) NEVER fired stage transitions. Hard-crashes at SOC 57-63%, VCELL ~3.36-3.45V every test. battery_health_log empty across all 4. Root cause: MAX17048 SOC% calibration is 40-percentage-points off (gauge=60% when VCELL=4.200V/full); SOC%-based thresholds (US-216's 30/25/20) cannot match real battery state. Sprint 19 fix: switch the orchestrator's trigger source from SOC% to VCELL (volts), with thresholds aligned to actual measured drain behavior. After this lands, the next drain should fire WARNING/IMMINENT/TRIGGER + populate battery_health_log.

stories.title: UpsMonitor BATTERY-detection fix: drop CRATE rule, harden VCELL slope
stories.intent: Compounds with US-234. Across 4 drain tests, UpsMonitor.getPowerSource() NEVER flipped from EXTERNAL to BATTERY when wall power was unplugged. Root cause: the CRATE register on this MAX17048 hardware setup is unreliable (Spool audit confirms returns 0xFFFF/disabled value). The fallback VCELL-slope rule isn't catching the actual drain trend either. Sprint 19 fix: drop CRATE-based rule entirely; replace with a more robust VCELL-based detection: VCELL drops below 3.95V sustained for 30s OR VCELL slope < -0.005V/min over 60s rolling window → BATTERY. After this lands + US-234 ships, getPowerSource() actually flips to BATTERY when wall power is removed.

stories.title: Actually fix US-228: drive_summary cold-start metadata via defer-INSERT (Option a)
stories.intent: Sprint 18's US-228 shipped Option (b) backfill-UPDATE for drive_summary cold-start metadata. Empirically across drives 3, 4, 5: every drive_summary row created post-Sprint-18 has all three sensor metadata fields (ambient_temp_at_start_c, starting_battery_v, barometric_kpa_at_start) as NULL. The UPDATE-backfill never fires — either it's not subscribed to the right event or has a logic bug only triggered by production ordering. Sprint 19 fix: switch to Option (a) defer-INSERT-until-first-reading approach. SummaryRecorder waits for first IAT/BATTERY_V/BARO reading to arrive (with bounded timeout) before INSERTing the drive_summary row, ensuring all three fields are populated at insertion time. Hard-fail the deferred path if the timeout expires with no readings — that's a real ECU silence event and drive_summary should reflect that with explicit-NULL + warning.

filesToTouch: src/pi/obdii/drive_summary.py::SummaryRecorder.captureDriveStart (replace UPDATE-backfill with defer-INSERT: register a one-shot reading-listener; first IAT/BATTERY_V/BARO reading received triggers the INSERT; if all 3 readings present, INSERT immediately; bounded-timeout 60s; on timeout, INSERT row with NULL fields and explicit reason='no_readings_within_timeout')
src/pi/obdii/data/logger.py (expose `subscribeNextReading(parameter_name, callback)` if not already; SummaryRecorder uses this for the listener)
src/pi/obdii/drive/detector.py (drive-start hook: call captureDriveStart but no longer expect the row to exist immediately)

invariants: Do NOT INSERT drive_summary immediately on drive_start. INSERT must wait for first reading OR timeout.
60s timeout is hard upper bound. Do NOT extend it dynamically.
On timeout, INSERT row with explicit reason; do NOT silently skip the INSERT.
Warm-restart rule from US-206: ambient_temp_at_start_c=NULL on warm restart, regardless of available readings.
Re-entry safety: a drive_start firing while a previous drive's listener is pending must NOT cause double-INSERT or orphan listener; previous pending listener cancels.
Synthetic test must FAIL against Sprint 18's Option (b) UPDATE-backfill code — that proves the test is strong enough to expose the original bug class.
stopConditions: STOP if subscribeNextReading API doesn't exist on ObdDataLogger and adding it requires significant refactor — file inbox note with refactor scope.
STOP if listener cancellation on drive-start re-entry creates a race condition (listener fires for old drive AFTER new drive_start) — must be deterministic.
STOP if 60s timeout doesn't fire reliably in tests (test environment uses real time.sleep) — use mocked clock per existing test pattern.

stories.title: V-1 drive_summary 3-way schema reconciliation (server 0004 migration + namespace cleanup)
stories.intent: Ralph's Drive 4 health check found 148 drive_summary sync failures (server returns HTTP 500 every time): server ORM model expects source_id+source_device columns; server actual table has device_id, start_time, end_time, duration_seconds (Sprint 7-8 analytics shape from sim test data); Pi schema has drive_id+ambient/baro/battery + data_source. Sprint 18 US-214 partial reconciliation didn't reach server tables. Sprint 19 fix: 0004 migration on server adds source_id+source_device columns to drive_summary AND truncates the 9 legacy Sprint 7-8 sim rows (per CIO directive 2026-04-29 — sim data has zero ongoing analytics value, matches US-205/US-227 'legacy ops cleanup' precedent); cascade-truncate drive_statistics rows with FK to legacy drive_summary ids 1-10 (computed_at=2026-04-16). After migration, retroactive sync of drive_3/4/5 drive_summary rows from Pi succeeds (rows are still all-NULL until US-236 ships, but schema is correct + sync HTTP 200).

stories.title: V-2 dtc_log server-side migration (0005) -- close silent-data-loss risk
stories.intent: US-204 (Sprint 15) added dtc_log to Pi for DTC retrieval (Mode 03/07). Server has no dtc_log table per Ralph's Drive 4 health check (SHOW TABLES confirms). Drive 4 had DTC_COUNT=0 so no data lost yet, but the next drive that hits a DTC will have the dtc_log row land on Pi only — silent data loss risk. Sprint 19 fix: 0005 migration on server creates dtc_log mirror with same shape as Pi (per src/pi/obdii/dtc_client.py / US-204). Add dtc_log to server's DELTA_SYNC_TABLES so sync flows.

stories.title: V-3 connection_log Drive 4 disambiguation: writer regression vs sync gap
stories.intent: Drive 4 should have generated connect_attempt + drive_start + drive_end events in connection_log per US-229 design. Server has zero Drive 4 entries (last connection_log entry on server is 2026-04-28T11:53). Two possible root causes Ralph couldn't disambiguate without Pi state: (a) Pi connection_log writer regressed post-Sprint-15 — drive_start/end events aren't being written; (b) Pi wrote them but didn't sync before unplug at ~14:00. Sprint 19 fix: at story-start, query Pi connection_log directly (MAX(id), MAX(timestamp), drive_id=4 entries). If Pi has them, path-b (sync gap, self-resolves on next sync) — file info note + close. If Pi doesn't have them, path-a (writer regression) — investigate event_router._handleDriveStart / _handleDriveEnd for missing connection_log INSERT calls + add them back.

filesToTouch: (IF path-a) src/pi/obdii/event_router.py or wherever drive lifecycle events route — add missing connection_log INSERT calls for drive_start + drive_end + connect_attempt; ensure event_type strings match the canonical CHECK constraint

(IF path-b) NO code changes; document finding in completionNotes + file info-only note
specs/architecture.md (if path-a, document the writer regression + fix)

stories.title: V-5 server-side orphan-row backfill (mirror US-233 Pi-side script for server)
stories.intent: Server has 541 NULL drive_id real rows (Ralph's V-5 finding): 269 pre-Drive-4 boundary + 348 in 13:00-14:00 Drive-4 hour + ~79 post-engine-off adapter polls. Pi-side US-233 backfill ran on Pi only; server-side equivalent never written. Sprint 19 fix: add server-side backfill script using server's MariaDB cursor pattern. Same algorithm as US-233: orphan → nearest subsequent drive_id within window. Post-engine-off adapter-poll rows (after drive_end) are explicit design decision: stay NULL (they're not part of any drive).

filesToTouch: scripts/backfill_server_premint_orphans.py (NEW -- mirror of scripts/backfill_premint_orphans.py from US-233 but operating against server MariaDB; UPDATE realtime_data SET drive_id = <nearest_subsequent_drive_id> WHERE drive_id IS NULL AND data_source='real' AND timestamp >= drive_start_minus_window AND timestamp < drive_start; bounded window 60s; --dry-run + --execute flags; idempotent)
scripts/backfill_server_premint_orphans.py (post-engine-off rows: stay NULL; explicit branch in script that does NOT match these rows; document explicitly)

tests/scripts/test_backfill_server_premint_orphans.py (NEW -- mock MariaDB with synthetic orphan rows; assert correct backfill associations; assert post-engine-off rows stay NULL; assert idempotent re-run)
specs/architecture.md (note about server-side orphan policy: pre-mint rows backfilled to subsequent drive within 60s window; post-engine-off rows stay NULL by design)
docs/testing.md (operator-facing doc on running the backfill script when needed)

scripts/apply_server_migrations.py (existing server-side script with MariaDB cursor pattern Ralph can mirror)

groundingRefs.value: 541 NULL drive_id real rows on server (269 pre-Drive-4 + 348 Drive-4-hour + 79 post-engine-off)
60s window (orphan timestamp ≥ drive_start - 60s) — match US-233 algorithm
Post-engine-off adapter-poll rows (after drive_end) stay NULL by explicit design
--dry-run mandatory; --execute requires prior --dry-run sentinel (US-205/US-227 pattern)

acceptance: Pre-flight: confirm server has ~541 NULL drive_id real rows; document the breakdown.
Script created at scripts/backfill_server_premint_orphans.py; mirrors Pi-side pattern from US-233; uses server MariaDB cursor.
--dry-run mode: scans, prints planned UPDATE counts, exits 0 without changes.
--execute mode: requires prior --dry-run sentinel; runs UPDATE in transaction; backs up affected rows via mysqldump-style snapshot first; rollback on failure.
Post-engine-off rows (timestamp > drive_end of last drive) explicitly excluded from match; stay NULL.

Synthetic test in test_backfill_server_premint_orphans.py: mocks MariaDB with synthetic orphan rows; asserts pre-mint rows backfilled correctly; asserts post-engine-off rows stay NULL; idempotent (re-run UPDATEs zero rows).
Post-execute on test data: SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE drive_id IS NULL AND data_source='real' returns only the 79-ish post-engine-off rows (or whatever count is appropriate).
specs/architecture.md updated.
Fast suite + new tests pass; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors.
invariants: Script is IDEMPOTENT: re-running on already-backfilled DB UPDATEs zero rows.
Backup MUST land before any UPDATE; fail open on backup failure.
Post-engine-off rows stay NULL by design — script's WHERE clause must explicitly exclude them.
60s window is hard upper bound for matching; older orphans stay NULL.
Do NOT touch Pi-side data; server-side only.
stopConditions: STOP if 541 row count is significantly different at story start (e.g., grew to 5000+ after another drive) — document and proceed; numbers will shift but algorithm stays.
STOP if synthetic test produces zero matches (algorithm bug) — investigate; backfill must associate at least the 269 pre-Drive-4 rows.
STOP if backup-first step exceeds reasonable time bound (>30s for 541 rows is suspicious) — investigate, but normally instant.

stories.title: B-047 US-A: Deploy versioning V0.18.0 + structured release record
stories.intent: Prerequisite for B-047 self-update feature (US-B/C/D in Sprint 20+). CIO directive 2026-04-29: SemVer-shaped V<major>.<minor>.<patch> starting at V0.18.0 (we have not had a stable V1.0.0 yet); each deploy writes a structured release record with version + UTC timestamp + description (≤400 char). Records land on each tier (Pi + server) at a known path (e.g., .deploy-version JSON file). Without this, B-047's self-update cannot compare versions; without it, every deploy is anonymous (just a git hash). After this lands, we have a structured release ledger going forward.

filesToTouch: deploy/RELEASE_VERSION (NEW -- canonical file at repo root tracking current version, format JSON: {"version":"V0.18.0", "description":"..."}; description bumped per release; updated by deploy scripts at deploy time but committed by PM at sprint close, not by deploy)

deploy/deploy-pi.sh (new step: read deploy/RELEASE_VERSION + git rev-parse --short HEAD + UTC timestamp; write JSON to /home/mcornelison/Projects/Eclipse-01/.deploy-version on Pi: {version, releasedAt, gitHash, description})
deploy/deploy-server.sh (mirror: write JSON to /mnt/projects/O/OBD2v2/.deploy-version on server)

scripts/version_helpers.py (NEW -- shared Python helpers: parseVersion, bumpVersion(major|minor|patch), validateRelease(json), readDeployVersion(path); used by both Pi and server tier when querying their installed version)

tests/deploy/test_release_versioning.py (NEW -- synthetic test that mocks deploy script flow: runs both deploy scripts in --dry-run mode; asserts they would write correctly-shaped JSON files; tests the version-bump logic; tests parsing of malformed records)
specs/architecture.md (new Section: Release Versioning + Deploy Records)
docs/testing.md (operator-facing: how to read .deploy-version on either tier; how to bump RELEASE_VERSION at sprint close)
doNotTouch: Existing deploy step ordering / behavior (only ADD the version-write step, don't reshape)
git commit / tag operations (per CIO rule: no git commands in sprint stories)
.env files or secrets
Existing deploy/ subdirectory addresses.sh, eclipse-obd.service etc. (untouched)

groundingRefs.value: Versioning scheme: V<major>.<minor>.<patch>
Starting version: V0.18.0 (post-Sprint-18)
Release record: {version, releasedAt (UTC ISO-8601), gitHash, description ≤400 char}
Deploy writes record on each tier; PM bumps RELEASE_VERSION at sprint close
Major bump = breaking schema/API changes; Minor = sprint completes new feature; Patch = bug fixes / hotfixes

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: confirm deploy/RELEASE_VERSION doesn't exist yet; confirm deploy scripts don't already write any version files.

deploy/RELEASE_VERSION created at repo root with content {"version":"V0.18.0", "description":"Sprint 18 ops-hardening shipped + Sprint 19 runtime fixes loading"} (or whatever description fits — 400 char max).
scripts/version_helpers.py exposes: parseVersion(str)→(major,minor,patch); bumpVersion(version,kind)→str; validateRelease(json)→bool; readDeployVersion(path)→record_dict_or_None.
deploy-pi.sh: new step writes JSON to /home/mcornelison/Projects/Eclipse-01/.deploy-version on every default deploy run (idempotent on unchanged version + git hash).
deploy-server.sh: same on server side, /mnt/projects/O/OBD2v2/.deploy-version.
Synthetic test test_release_versioning.py: runs both scripts in --dry-run mode; captures the JSON they would write; validates shape against validateRelease.
Bump version logic test: parseVersion("V0.18.0") → (0,18,0); bumpVersion("V0.18.0", "minor") → "V0.19.0"; bumpVersion("V0.18.0", "major") → "V1.0.0"; bumpVersion("V0.18.0", "patch") → "V0.18.1".
Description ≤400 char validation: validateRelease rejects records with description >400 char.
specs/architecture.md new Section documents the versioning scheme + release record shape + how the tiers query their installed version.
Fast suite + new tests pass; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors.
verification: ruff check scripts/version_helpers.py deploy/deploy-pi.sh deploy/deploy-server.sh tests/deploy/test_release_versioning.py
invariants: Version string always starts with capital V (e.g., V0.18.0 not v0.18.0).
Description hard limit 400 char; validateRelease rejects longer.
Deploy scripts NEVER bump RELEASE_VERSION themselves — that's PM action at sprint close. Deploy writes the CURRENT version + timestamp + hash to .deploy-version on tier.
JSON shape: {version, releasedAt, gitHash, description}; releasedAt is UTC ISO-8601 (e.g., '2026-04-30T14:32:00Z').
Idempotent: re-running deploy with same version+gitHash overwrites tier .deploy-version with refreshed releasedAt timestamp (so we know last-deployed-at) but doesn't otherwise change.
Do NOT git commit deploy/RELEASE_VERSION inside the deploy script — committing is PM's job at sprint close.
B-047 US-B/C/D consume this ledger; do NOT block them on this story changing shape — keep API stable from US-A onward.
stopConditions: STOP if deploy/RELEASE_VERSION needs to be a different format than JSON (e.g., simpler key=value) for some tier — file inbox note; default to JSON.
STOP if existing deploy scripts have a `--version` flag or some prior version mechanism — reconcile, don't bypass.
STOP if writing .deploy-version on the Pi requires elevated permissions — should be writable by mcornelison; if not, check ownership.
